solution-folder.name: ULTIMATE CALL BLOCKER - FAQs
solution-folder.description: Below are the most common questions and answers about the Ultimate Call Blocker. If you still need help, or can't find the answer you are looking for, please use "Contact" us via our main website".

solution-article.title: Step-by-step UBlocker Setup
solution-article.description:
Congratulations!!! on your purchase of a Ultimate Call Blocker (UBlocker).  Please follow this step by step instructions to setup your UBlocker. These instructions apply to UBlocker LAN and WIFI versions.

Step 1.  Connecting your phoneline

a. Locate the device labeled as "Phone Adapter" in the package. 

– Allows the UBlocker to connect to your landline phone jack or modem port.
 
– Plugs into any of the four USB ports on the UBlocker.
 
– Includes two RJ11 sockets:

 • LINE: Connects to your wall jack or modem port.

 • PHONE: Connects to your phone or phone system.

 – An RJ11 patch cord is included. 


b. Connect the USB plug on the Phone Adapter to any the 4 USB ports available in the UBlocker (as shown in diagram below).  

c. On the other side of the Phone Adapter, there are 2 phone sockets (or holes e.g., RJ11 sockets).  One labeled "LINE", and the other labeled "PHONE".   Plug in a phone cable (one is included in the package) to the socket labeled "LINE" on the Phone Adapter.  Then, plug in the other side of the phone cable to the phone line (e.g., wall jack or modem port). 


d. Next, you can plug in your existing phone system (handset or base) to the socket labeled "PHONE" on the Phone Adapter. 


Important:  This know that it is not required to have a phone connected to the Phone Adapter of the UBlocker in order for the UBlocker to work properly, however you will need a phone connected to the phone adapter in order to make sure you have dial tone going to the Phone Adapter.  After you finish the UBlocker setup, you can unplug the phone and use it on any other wall jack, if desired.



Important:

  • The Phone Adapter has two RJ11 sockets labeled LINE and PHONE.

  • Plug the LINE port into the wall jack or modem port (the one with a dial tone) using an RJ11 telephone patch cable.

  • If desired, connect your existing telephone (base or handset) to the PHONE port using another RJ11 patch cable (existing).

  • A phone connection is optional — the UBlocker will still function correctly even without one connected.

  • The Phone Adapter has a red led ON at all times indicating that it has power. It also has an intermittent green led ON indicating activity (off hook, answering or making calls).

Step 2.  Powering your UBlocker device

Plug the Power Adapter (included) into the micro USB port on the UBlocker, as shown below.
Then, connect the power adapter to a standard 120V wall outlet. 



Important:

  • For best performance and protection, connect the UBlocker to a UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply).
     → This helps prevent damage and extends the life of your device during power interruptions.

  • Some power adapter models have a red led light ON indicating power is ON.

  • Recommendation: If order to protect your UBlocker equipment as well as extend its lifespan, we recommend you use a UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply) to plug in the power adapter.  If a UPS is not available, use a surge-protected outlet to guard against power surges.

Step 3. Connecting your UBlocker to the Internet

Your UBlocker can connect to the Internet in two ways:

  1. LAN / Ethernet

  2. Wi-Fi

Note: We recommend you use a LAN / Ethernet connection in order to provide a more reliable connection.


LAN / Ethernet Setup


Follow these steps to connect your UBlocker directly to your router using an Ethernet cable.
If you have a UBlocker version WIFI or WL and need to use a Wi-Fi connection, you may skip this section and go to Step 4.

  1. Take the included Ethernet cable and plug one end into your router.
    - Your router (or modem) should have at least 4 Ethernet ports available to allow connecting other devices e.g., desktop PC, laptop, TV, etc.  

  2. Plug the other end into the Ethernet port on your UBlocker.



Step 4. WIFI setup (optional, only apply to UBlocker WIFI or WL models)

PLEASE SKIP THIS STEP IF YOU ALREADY HAVE YOUR UBLOCKER CONNECTED DIRECTLY TO YOUR ROUTER WITH AN ETHERNET CABLE (e.g. completed Step 3 above).

Please connect your UBlocker to the Internet via Wi-Fi only if you are not able to connect your UBlocker directly to your router with an Ethernet cable.


Important:  Please make sure your UBlocker supports WIFI or WL. If you have a UBlocker LAN version, please skip this step.

Preparation:

  • You need a Wi-Fi-enabled device (smartphone, tablet, or laptop).

  • You will also need your Wi-Fi router’s SSID and password to complete the setup.

    • The SSID is the name of your router as it appears under “My Networks” or “Other Networks” on your Wi-Fi-enabled device.

    • Important: The SSID is case-sensitive, so type it exactly as shown.

    • If your router’s SSID is very long, it may be truncated in the network list. In that case, check the label on your router or contact your Internet Service Provider (ISP) for the full name.

      ⚠️ Caution: Most Wi-Fi setup failures occur because the SSID is entered incorrectly. 

  • Important: If you connected your UBlocker to your router with an Ethernet cable, unplug the Ethernet cable from the UBlocker before starting the Wi-Fi setup. You can reconnect it after you finish the Wi-Fi setup, if desired.


4a) Connect to the UBlocker Network

  1. On your Wi-Fi device, go to Settings > Wi-Fi.

  2. Wait for a network named “UBlocker” to appear under available networks.

  3. Select UBlocker and enter the password: ultimateblocker

  4. Once connected, close the Wi-Fi settings.

Note: It may show “No Internet” — this is normal.


4b) Configure your home Wi-Fi

  1. Open a browser (e.g., Safari, Chrome, Firefox, IE) on your Wi-Fi device.

  2. Enter http://192.168.2.1 in the address bar and press Enter.

  3. On the page that appears, enter your Wi-Fi router’s SSID and password.

  4. Press the “Update Wi-Fi” button.

Important: Both SSID and password are case-sensitive. Enter the SSID (e.g. name) of your router exactly as it appears in your WIFI available networks list.


Please type your WIFI router’s SSID and Password of your WIFI router and press the “Update Wi-Fi” button. Important:  Please make sure you type the SSID exactly as you see it under “Available networks”.  Both entries are case sensitive.

Tips & Troubleshooting

  1. After powering up your UBlocker for the first time, it may take a few minutes (up to 15 minutes) for “UBlocker” to appear in your network list. Please be patient.

  2. Make sure to type “http://” before 192.168.2.1 in step 4b.

  3. Ignore browser messages like “This site can’t be reached” after pressing Update Wi-Fi. At this point, your UBlocker is switching from your device to your Wi-Fi router.

  4. Complete this setup within 5 minutes. Otherwise, the UBlocker will reboot and you may need to redo the process.

  5. Once the UBlocker connects to your Wi-Fi router, “UBlocker” will no longer appear in your device’s available networks.

Step 5. Creating your online account & adding your UBlocker device

Next, you need to create an online account to manage your UBlocker.

  1. On any Internet-connected device (PC, laptop, smartphone, or tablet), go to:
    https://ultimatecallblocker.com

  2. First-time users: Click the Sign Up link below the Username & Password fields to create a new account.  Fill out the registration form and press Submit.

     

  3. After registering, log in using your Username and Password.

     

  4. Once logged in, click the “Add New Device” button to register your UBlocker to your account.

Tip: Keep your account credentials safe — you’ll need them to manage your UBlocker and make future changes.




Note: The Alias field is optional (useful if you manage multiple devices).  

Enter your UBlocker’s ID & Label Key (Refer to the sheet titled "Ultimate Call Blocker (UBlocker) Registration Codes & Installation Steps" included with your UBlocker showing your device's registration codes.  It should look like this:
ID: xxxxxxxxx    Label Key: xxxxxxxx

Warning:  The Key and Label Key codes are case sensitive.  Please be extra careful typing these 2 sets of codes.  

Important: You can find your UBlocker’s ID and Key on the instruction sheets included in the package with your UBlocker device. These codes are unique to your UBlocker and are required for account registration and device management.  


Tip:  If after entering these codes online, your device is still not showing, please contact our technical support team so we can assist you. 

⚠️ If you lose the instruction sheets containing your ID and Key before you can register your device online, a factory reset will be required. In that case, you must mail your UBlocker to our main office for servicing.


Step 6. Testing your device's setup

After completing the setup above, follow these simple instructions to make sure your UBlocker is installed and working properly.

  1. Use your mobile phone or another phone line to make a test call to the number connected to your UBlocker.

  2. Let the call ring 2–3 times, then hang up.

  3. Log in to your account at https://ultimatecallblocker.com and press Refresh on your browser.

  4. Scroll to the “Call Log” section and check if your test call has been recorded.

✅ If your test call appears in the Call Log, your UBlocker is set up correctly — congratulations! You have completed the setup of your UBlocker.

Troubleshooting

If the test call is not logged, follow these steps:

  1. Ensure the phone line is connected properly.  

    1. Refer to step 1 above to review the Phone Adapter connections.  

    2. Plug in your phone to the PHONE socket on the Phone Adapter and make sure you hear dial tone.

  2. Verify that the UBlocker is properly connected to the Internet.

    1. If you are using an Ethernet cable to connect the UBlocker directly into your router, make sure you have a blinking green light (activity light) on both; the UBlocker's Ethernet port (check LEDs below the socket), and also on your router.  

    2. If you can, please log to your router and check the connected devices to make sure there is one showing as "UBlocker".  (Note:  This may required help from your Internet service provider.)  

    3. If you have another device connected to your router via Ethernet cable, try swapping the ports to see if you get connectivity as some ISPs may disable unused Ethernet ports).

    4. If you are using a Wi-Fi connection, please check your Wi-Fi enabled device to make sure the UBlocker is not showing under "My Networks" or "Other Networks".  If the UBlocker is still showing, this means that your UBlocker is not connecting to the Internet yet.  Please go over step 4 above again and make sure you enter the correct SSID and password.

  3. Power cycle your UBlocker device (unplug the power adapter, wait 30 seconds and then plug it back on).

If the problem persists after these steps, contact our technical support team here

  • Include your name, phone number, best time to call, and login info (username or registered email).

  • A technician will assist you over the phone, usually within 1 business day.


What’s Next?

Once Your UBlocker Is Set Up and Tested

Your device is now fully functional and will begin blocking unwanted calls automatically.
The UBlocker uses our Global Blacklist, which contains over 500,000 known scam and fraud numbers — and it continues to grow daily.
Any call from one of these numbers will be blocked automatically and recorded in your Call Log for your reference.
We recommend allowing your UBlocker to receive incoming calls for a few days so it can start building your New List.  This list records all phone numbers that have recently called you. Once calls appear in your New List, you can review them and decide whether to move them to your Blacklist (to block future calls) or to your Whitelist (to allow calls from trusted numbers).


Simply wait for calls to come in and manage your lists anytime at https://ultimatecallblocker.com.

Click here to learn how to manage your UBlocker.


If you have any questions on how to manage your account online, please refer to the ULTIMATE CALL BLOCKER FAQs or Contact us.

solution-article.title: What is included with my UBlocker?
solution-article.description:
Your UBlocker package includes the following:

(1) UBlocker Device
– The main unit, powered by a Raspberry Pi mini computer.


(1) Power Adapter
– Provides power to the UBlocker.
– Includes a micro USB plug (connects to the UBlocker) and a standard U.S. plug (for any 120V outlet).


(1) Phone Adapter (USB Modem)
– Allows the UBlocker to connect to your landline phone jack or modem port.
– Plugs into any of the four USB ports on the UBlocker.
– Includes two RJ11 sockets:
 • LINE: Connects to your wall jack or modem port.
 • PHONE: Connects to your phone or phone system.
– An RJ11 patch cord is included.


(1) Ethernet Patch Cable with Two RJ45 Plugs
– Used to connect the UBlocker directly to your router.
– A wired connection is highly recommended for the most reliable Internet performance.


(1) Documentation (Installation Instructions & User Manual)
– Step-by-step setup guide for your UBlocker device.
– Contains your UBlocker’s unique ID and Key (case-sensitive).
– These codes are required to link your device to your online account.

(1) Power Adapter – Provides power to the UBlocker. – Includes a micro USB plug (connects to the UBlocker) and a standard U.S. plug (for any 120V outlet). (1) Phone Adapter (USB Modem) – Allows the UBlocker to connect to your landline phone jack or modem port. – Plugs into any of the four USB ports on the UBlocker. – Includes two RJ11 sockets: • LINE: Connects to your wall jack or modem port. • PHONE: Connects to your phone or phone system. – An RJ11 patch cord is included. (1) Ethernet Patch Cable with Two RJ45 Plugs – Used to connect the UBlocker directly to your router. – A wired connection is highly recommended for the most reliable Internet performance. (1) Documentation (Installation Instructions & User Manual) – Step-by-step setup guide for your UBlocker device. – Contains your UBlocker’s unique ID and Key (case-sensitive). – These codes are required to link your device to your online account.

solution-article.title: How to block calls using a numbers or names?
solution-article.description:
To manage your blocked and allowed numbers, log in to your account at:
? https://ultimatecallblocker.com/login

Our user interface is designed to be simple, intuitive, and easy to use.
You can block or allow numbers and names in the following ways:

1) Add Entries Manually

Use the “Add Entry” section to add numbers or names directly to your Blacklist or Whitelist.

  • You can enter full or partial numbers/names.

  • Choose the Match Type: Exact Match, Contains, Starts With, or Ends With.

  • Select the List Type you want to add the entry to (Blacklist or Whitelist).



Important:

  • We strongly recommend avoiding the manual method for building your call lists. Instead, use the approach described in Section 2 to create your Blacklist and Whitelist automatically.

    Why?
    When you add numbers manually, you must know exactly how the phone numbers are registered on your phone line. Even small differences can prevent entries from working correctly.

    For example:

    • If you manually enter a local 10-digit number such as 305-512-1234, but your phone system registers calls as 1-305-512-1234, the block will not take effect.

    • The same applies to names. If you want to block calls with “FLORIDA” in the name field, but your incoming calls display only “FL” or “FLO,” the entry will not match and will fail to block the call.

  • By using the method in Section 2, your UBlocker automatically recognizes how numbers and names are logged, helping you avoid these common errors.


2) Move Calls from the New List

You can also block or allow numbers directly from your New List:

  1. Open the New List section.

  2. Click the buttons labeled “Move to Whitelist” or “Move to Blacklist” under each number.


Important:

  • We highly recommend you use this method to build your Blacklist and Whitelist instead of the doing it manually. 
  • The ability to move calls from your New List becomes available as incoming calls start populating your New List. So, when you first setup your UBlocker you will not be able to do this.  Just wait a few days so that new calls come in, and then you will be able to start building your Blacklist and Whitelist. 



3) Use the Global Blacklist Search

Search for a number in the Global Blacklist Search section to see if it’s already listed in our global database.

  • If the number appears in the Global Blacklist, it is automatically blocked — you do not need to add it again.

  • If you want to receive calls from a number that appears on the Global Blacklist, add it to your Whitelist to override the global filter.

If you want to receive calls from a number that appears on the Global Blacklist, add it to your Whitelist to override the global filter.

solution-article.title: How does the ULTIMATE CALL BLOCKER works?
solution-article.description: When a call comes in, the UBlocker automatically connects to HQTelecom’s secure cloud server to verify the caller’s number. It checks three lists: the Global Blacklist, your Personal Blacklist, and your Whitelist.  If the number appears on any blacklist, the call is disconnected within 1–2 seconds, stopping unwanted calls before you even pick up.
solution-article.desc-un-html: When a call comes in, the UBlocker automatically connects to HQTelecom’s secure cloud server to verify the caller’s number. It checks three lists: the Global Blacklist, your Personal Blacklist, and your Whitelist. If the number appears on any blacklist, the call is disconnected within 1–2 seconds, stopping unwanted calls before you even pick up.

solution-article.title: Can the UBlocker work by itself? Even if I don't manage my lists?
solution-article.description: Yes! Once setup, UBlocker works automatically without having to manage your lists.  Since the UBlocker has over 500K numbers in the Global Blacklist, it will automatically block all calls from these blacklisted numbers linked to scams, frauds, and unsolicited calls.
solution-article.desc-un-html: Yes! Once setup, UBlocker works automatically without having to manage your lists. Since the UBlocker has over 500K numbers in the Global Blacklist, it will automatically block all calls from these blacklisted numbers linked to scams, frauds, and unsolicited calls.

solution-article.title: What is required for the UBlocker to work?
solution-article.description:
- A landline phone line (can be POTs, cooper-based, Cable-based, or VoIP).
- Caller ID service active (e.g. the ability to read name & phone numbers on your existing handsets). 

- An Internet connection (UBlocker version LAN requires an ethernet connection on your router or switch, UBlocker WL can connect via WIFI).
- You also need a regular phone or handset with CallerID display
- You also need a PC, smartphone or tablet to manage your device online.
solution-article.desc-un-html: - A landline phone line (can be POTs, cooper-based, Cable-based, or VoIP). - Caller ID service active (e.g. the ability to read name & phone numbers on your existing handsets). - An Internet connection (UBlocker version LAN requires an ethernet connection on your router or switch, UBlocker WL can connect via WIFI). - You also need a regular phone or handset with CallerID display - You also need a PC, smartphone or tablet to manage your device online.

solution-article.title: How do I login to manage my device?
solution-article.description:
Please login at the website https://ultimatecallblocker.com


If you still need help, please contact us
solution-article.desc-un-html: Please login at the website https://ultimatecallblocker.com If you still need help, please contact us

solution-article.title: How can I manage the lists?
solution-article.description: You can use any Internet-enable device (PC, smartphone or tablet) to login to your https://ultimatecallblocker.com/login and manage your lists.  You can login from anywhere and manage your lists (Blacklist and Whitelist).
solution-article.desc-un-html: You can use any Internet-enable device (PC, smartphone or tablet) to login to your https://ultimatecallblocker.com/login and manage your lists. You can login from anywhere and manage your lists (Blacklist and Whitelist).

solution-article.title: I can't connect to 192.168.2.1?
solution-article.description:
First of all, you don't connect to 192.168.2.1, you browse to 192.168.2.1 using a browser in your PC or smartphone AFTER you have JOINed to the ublocker as your WIFI network.  So, between steps 1a) and 1b), you can check your wifi network to make sure you are connected to ublocker as your wifi network.  If so, you should see that the IP address for the router is 192.168.2.1 ...when you click the information icon on the WIFI network.




After you enter your home (or office) SSID & password, the ublocker should restart in the normal mode.

If the internet connection is lost or it can't connect to HQTelecom's remote server for some reason (e.g. user entered wrong ssid or password) it will keep trying to reconnect for 3 minutes, and if it can't couldn't it will go back to hotspot mode again and the user should be able to browse to 192.168.2.1 and re-enter the ssid and password.  

The ublocker will go back to hotspot mode (e.g. able to browse to 192.168.2.1) for 10 minutes and then it'll check the old network (e.g. previously entered SSID & password) again.  It will continue to do this until it can connect to HQTelecom's remote server.


Hope this is helpful.  If you need more help, please contact us.
solution-article.desc-un-html: First of all, you don't connect to 192.168.2.1, you browse to 192.168.2.1 using a browser in your PC or smartphone AFTER you have JOINed to the ublocker as your WIFI network. So, between steps 1a) and 1b), you can check your wifi network to make sure you are connected to ublocker as your wifi network. If so, you should see that the IP address for the router is 192.168.2.1 ...when you click the information icon on the WIFI network. After you enter your home (or office) SSID & password, the ublocker should restart in the normal mode. If the internet connection is lost or it can't connect to HQTelecom's remote server for some reason (e.g. user entered wrong ssid or password) it will keep trying to reconnect for 3 minutes, and if it can't couldn't it will go back to hotspot mode again and the user should be able to browse to 192.168.2.1 and re-enter the ssid and password. The ublocker will go back to hotspot mode (e.g. able to browse to 192.168.2.1) for 10 minutes and then it'll check the old network (e.g. previously entered SSID & password) again. It will continue to do this until it can connect to HQTelecom's remote server. Hope this is helpful. If you need more help, please contact us.

solution-article.title: How to suppress the ring(s) on blocked calls when using my Ultimate Call Blocker
solution-article.description:
How the UBlocker Works with First-Ring Suppression


When a call comes in from a number on the Blacklist, the UBlocker requires 2–3 seconds to read the Caller ID (name and number) and communicate with our server before disconnecting the call. During this process, the phone will usually ring once before the call is blocked.


At HQTelecom, we understand that even a single ring from a blocked call can be a nuisance. Our team has been searching for a solution to fully suppress these rings. While many products have claimed to do this, none worked effectively, except one — the Panasonic Model KX-TG994SK (and newer model KX-TGF973) series with base unit model KX-TGF970 includes a feature called “One Ring Scam” suppression. This feature eliminates the first one to two rings on all incoming calls. 


Our engineers at HQTelecom have tested the KX-TGF970 base units and confirmed that it successfully suppresses the first 1–2 rings when paired with UBlocker WiFi/LAN models running on Raspberry Pi 3B and 3B+. This short delay provides just enough time for the UBlocker to disconnect blacklisted calls (both user and global lists) before your phone ever rings.


Note: There are at least 2 variations of these Panasonic phone systems.  


Important Note for Older UBlocker Models
If you are using an older UBlocker WiFi or LAN unit, you may still experience a partial ring on some blacklisted calls—even with the Panasonic KX-TGF970 system. If this happens, please contact us for upgrade options to ensure full compatibility.


[ Click here to purchase a Panasonic Base Unit Model KX-TGF970 (refurbished) from our website ]


Why Use Panasonic KX-TGF970 Base Unit + Ultimate Call Blocker Together 
The Panasonic KX-TGF970 includes built-in call blocking features such as:

  • A database of 14,000 pre-loaded scam numbers

  • Storage for up to 2,000 additional blocked numbers

While helpful, these features cannot compare to the UBlocker’s advanced technology, which includes:

  • A Global Blacklist of over 500,000 pre-recorded scam numbers

  • Unlimited capacity for user-defined blacklists

  • Advanced options such as Whitelist Mode and partial name/number blocking

In summary: For the best protection against unwanted calls, we recommend combining Panasonic’s first-ring suppression feature with the powerful call blocking capabilities of the UBlocker.
solution-article.desc-un-html: How the UBlocker Works with First-Ring Suppression When a call comes in from a number on the Blacklist, the UBlocker requires 2–3 seconds to read the Caller ID (name and number) and communicate with our server before disconnecting the call. During this process, the phone will usually ring once before the call is blocked. At HQTelecom, we understand that even a single ring from a blocked call can be a nuisance. Our team has been searching for a solution to fully suppress these rings. While many products have claimed to do this, none worked effectively, except one — the Panasonic Model KX-TG994SK (and newer model KX-TGF973) series with base unit model KX-TGF970 includes a feature called “One Ring Scam” suppression. This feature eliminates the first one to two rings on all incoming calls. Our engineers at HQTelecom have tested the KX-TGF970 base units and confirmed that it successfully suppresses the first 1–2 rings when paired with UBlocker WiFi/LAN models running on Raspberry Pi 3B and 3B+. This short delay provides just enough time for the UBlocker to disconnect blacklisted calls (both user and global lists) before your phone ever rings. Note: There are at least 2 variations of these Panasonic phone systems. Important Note for Older UBlocker Models If you are using an older UBlocker WiFi or LAN unit, you may still experience a partial ring on some blacklisted calls—even with the Panasonic KX-TGF970 system. If this happens, please contact us for upgrade options to ensure full compatibility. [ Click here to purchase a Panasonic Base Unit Model KX-TGF970 (refurbished) from our website ] Why Use Panasonic KX-TGF970 Base Unit + Ultimate Call Blocker Together The Panasonic KX-TGF970 includes built-in call blocking features such as: A database of 14,000 pre-loaded scam numbers Storage for up to 2,000 additional blocked numbers While helpful, these features cannot compare to the UBlocker’s advanced technology, which includes: A Global Blacklist of over 500,000 pre-recorded scam numbers Unlimited capacity for user-defined blacklists Advanced options such as Whitelist Mode and partial name/number blocking In summary: For the best protection against unwanted calls, we recommend combining Panasonic’s first-ring suppression feature with the powerful call blocking capabilities of the UBlocker.

solution-article.title: How do I set a blocked phone number to be an allowed phone number?
solution-article.description:
Simply login to your account at https://ultimatecallblocker.com/login using any internet capable device (PC/MAC, tablet or cell phone). 


The easiest way to make sure you can receive calls from a particular phone number is simply by you adding the number or the name manually to your Whitelist by using the "Add Entry" section:

Important:  All the entries you add to your Whitelist will always overwrite (or supersede)  the entries in your BlackList or the Global BlackList.

Below are other ways to unblock a number:

If the number is being blocked, this number should be in the user BlackList or in our Global Blacklist. So, first search your User BlackList in the section called "Current Data", and search the number you want to allow from the BlackList.  Next, select it with your mouse, and click the button below called "Move to WhiteList"


If the number is not in your user BlackList, then type the number in the Global Backlist Section, and press "Search".
solution-article.desc-un-html: Simply login to your account at https://ultimatecallblocker.com/login using any internet capable device (PC/MAC, tablet or cell phone). The easiest way to make sure you can receive calls from a particular phone number is simply by you adding the number or the name manually to your Whitelist by using the "Add Entry" section: Important: All the entries you add to your Whitelist will always overwrite (or supersede) the entries in your BlackList or the Global BlackList. Below are other ways to unblock a number: If the number is being blocked, this number should be in the user BlackList or in our Global Blacklist. So, first search your User BlackList in the section called "Current Data", and search the number you want to allow from the BlackList. Next, select it with your mouse, and click the button below called "Move to WhiteList" If the number is not in your user BlackList, then type the number in the Global Backlist Section, and press "Search".

solution-article.title: Will my device stop working when my device's subscription expires?
solution-article.description:
Your UBlocker may continue to block calls, however you will not be able to manage your lists online unless you pay a yearly maintenance fee.  This fee allows us to cover the recurring costs associated with maintaining the website up and running, and also maintaining the Global Blacklist.   


Your device(s) will be restored and all data and lists will show again upon completing your payment. Please allow 1-2 business hours after completing your payment to get your account re-activated.
solution-article.desc-un-html: Your UBlocker may continue to block calls, however you will not be able to manage your lists online unless you pay a yearly maintenance fee. This fee allows us to cover the recurring costs associated with maintaining the website up and running, and also maintaining the Global Blacklist. Your device(s) will be restored and all data and lists will show again upon completing your payment. Please allow 1-2 business hours after completing your payment to get your account re-activated.

solution-article.title: Will I lose any of the numbers I have previously blocked?
solution-article.description: No, all the numbers (names and area codes) that are in your personal lists (Blacklist, Whitelist, and New) at the time your subscription expires will remain in your device, including the numbers initially detected and blocked by the Global Blacklist (as you may noticed, there are numbers in your personal Blacklist that were added automatically by HQTelecom's Global Blacklist service).  Again, we highly recommend that you renew your subscription as soon as possible in order to get the best possible protection.
solution-article.desc-un-html: No, all the numbers (names and area codes) that are in your personal lists (Blacklist, Whitelist, and New) at the time your subscription expires will remain in your device, including the numbers initially detected and blocked by the Global Blacklist (as you may noticed, there are numbers in your personal Blacklist that were added automatically by HQTelecom's Global Blacklist service). Again, we highly recommend that you renew your subscription as soon as possible in order to get the best possible protection.

solution-article.title: How does the Global Blacklist affect my UBlocker's functionality?
solution-article.description:
The Global Blacklist enhances your UBlocker's functionality by automatically blocking over 500,000 numbers (and growing at a rate of thousands daily) linked to scams and spams in the US and abroad. 


Our team is constantly adding new numbers linked to new unsolicited calls including scams, robocalls, political calls, etc.  


Our community of thousands of UBlocker owners also benefit from each other's efforts.  Our server is programmed to automatically add numbers to the Global BlackList when 2 or more users add a specific number.  So, it is not just you working to block unwanted calls -- Now you have a whole community doing this together!
solution-article.desc-un-html: The Global Blacklist enhances your UBlocker's functionality by automatically blocking over 500,000 numbers (and growing at a rate of thousands daily) linked to scams and spams in the US and abroad. Our team is constantly adding new numbers linked to new unsolicited calls including scams, robocalls, political calls, etc. Our community of thousands of UBlocker owners also benefit from each other's efforts. Our server is programmed to automatically add numbers to the Global BlackList when 2 or more users add a specific number. So, it is not just you working to block unwanted calls -- Now you have a whole community doing this together!

solution-article.title: How do I know when a call is blocked?
solution-article.description:
2 ways:

1) Online, your BLACKLIST shows all the numbers that have been blocked.  Remember that there are 2 types of numbers in the Blacklist: 1) Those that have been blocked by the Global Blacklist, and 2) those manually added by the users.  

2) You will hear 1-2 rings when the call is disconnected.  If you have a caller id phone, you will see the number in the display.
solution-article.desc-un-html: 2 ways: 1) Online, your BLACKLIST shows all the numbers that have been blocked. Remember that there are 2 types of numbers in the Blacklist: 1) Those that have been blocked by the Global Blacklist, and 2) those manually added by the users. 2) You will hear 1-2 rings when the call is disconnected. If you have a caller id phone, you will see the number in the display.

solution-article.title: What do the LEDs (flashing lights) on the UBlocker and Phone Adapter mean?
solution-article.description:
There are LEDs that light up on the UBlocker and also on the Phone Adapter.

UBlocker WIFI -- The LED on the circuit board will usually show in GREEN when the unit is in idle mode (e.g. ready to block calls).

UBlocker LAN -- The LED on the circuit board will usually show in RED when the unit is in idle mode (e.g. ready to block calls).  There are 2 LEDs on the Ethernet port.  The one on the left side should be GREEN (power) and the other should be GREEN or AMBER (activity) in idle mode.


Telephone Adapter -- The LED on the Telephone adapter should be RED in idle mode.
solution-article.desc-un-html: There are LEDs that light up on the UBlocker and also on the Phone Adapter. UBlocker WIFI -- The LED on the circuit board will usually show in GREEN when the unit is in idle mode (e.g. ready to block calls). UBlocker LAN -- The LED on the circuit board will usually show in RED when the unit is in idle mode (e.g. ready to block calls). There are 2 LEDs on the Ethernet port. The one on the left side should be GREEN (power) and the other should be GREEN or AMBER (activity) in idle mode. Telephone Adapter -- The LED on the Telephone adapter should be RED in idle mode.

solution-article.title: What's the difference between the Ultimate Call Blocker and Nomorobo?
solution-article.description:
There are some key differences between Nomorobo and the Ultimate Call Blocker.

1) Ultimate Call Blocker features a Global Blacklist service managed by HQTelecom and works with all providers (VoIP & Landline). It only requires Caller ID to work.  HQTelecom's Global Blacklist service currently has over 200,000 unsolicited phone numbers.


Nomorobo only works with some VoIP providers but not landline service providers such as AT&T, Verizon Standard, etc.
https://nomorobo.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/205761775-What-phone-carriers-are-supported-

2) In addition to caller id, Nomorobo also requires a special service called "Simultaneous ring" service -- this usually requires additional monthly cost.https://nomorobo.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/200536477-How-does-it-work-

Ultimate Call Blocker does not require any special service in addition to caller id.

3) Ultimate Call Blocker / Global Blacklist service includes scammers, and ALL unsolicited callers.  


The Nomorobo service focuses mostly on robocalls.


4) With the Ultimate Call Blocker , you can manage your phone lists online, including Blacklist, Whitelist, etc. 

Nomorobo does not offer any list management capability.

5) Whitelist Mode is a service mode that allows users to block all incoming calls except from those numbers in the user's whitelist.


Nomorobo does not offer any Whitelist services.


6) Name Blocking is a feature only available in the Ultimate Call Blocker.  This feature allows users to block incoming calls using names, in addition to phone numbers.  

Nomorobo does not offer name blocking.

It only requires Caller ID to work. HQTelecom's Global Blacklist service currently has over 200,000 unsolicited phone numbers. Nomorobo only works with some VoIP providers but not landline service providers such as AT&T, Verizon Standard, etc. https://nomorobo.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/205761775-What-phone-carriers-are-supported- 2) In addition to caller id, Nomorobo also requires a special service called "Simultaneous ring" service -- this usually requires additional monthly cost.https://nomorobo.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/200536477-How-does-it-work- Ultimate Call Blocker does not require any special service in addition to caller id. 3) Ultimate Call Blocker / Global Blacklist service includes scammers, and ALL unsolicited callers. The Nomorobo service focuses mostly on robocalls. 4) With the Ultimate Call Blocker , you can manage your phone lists online, including Blacklist, Whitelist, etc. Nomorobo does not offer any list management capability. 5) Whitelist Mode is a service mode that allows users to block all incoming calls except from those numbers in the user's whitelist. Nomorobo does not offer any Whitelist services. 6) Name Blocking is a feature only available in the Ultimate Call Blocker. This feature allows users to block incoming calls using names, in addition to phone numbers. Nomorobo does not offer name blocking.

solution-article.title: Can the UBlocker block call that show as "unavailable" also?
solution-article.description: Yes, you can block calls using any names e.g. "unavailable".
solution-article.desc-un-html: Yes, you can block calls using any names e.g. "unavailable".

solution-article.title: My phone company identifies some junk calls by prefixing the CID name with SPAM-XXXXXXX. How would I go about blocking all calls with a CID name starting with "SPAM"?
solution-article.description: A simple way to block all these calls is adding the name "SPAM" to the Blacklist as a NAME filter.  This will block all calls that have the SPAM in the CID name.  Also, please keep in mind that most likely these numbers are already registered in HQTelecom's Global Blacklist so even if you don't add this filter, 99% of these will be blocked.
solution-article.desc-un-html: A simple way to block all these calls is adding the name "SPAM" to the Blacklist as a NAME filter. This will block all calls that have the SPAM in the CID name. Also, please keep in mind that most likely these numbers are already registered in HQTelecom's Global Blacklist so even if you don't add this filter, 99% of these will be blocked.

solution-article.title: In the case of a power outage, will the UBlocker unit default to pass-through until power is restored?
solution-article.description: Yes, if the UBlocker is turned OFF due to a power failure or if the Internet is down, there will be no call blocking (e.g, pass-through) until power is restored and/or Internet is back online.
solution-article.desc-un-html: Yes, if the UBlocker is turned OFF due to a power failure or if the Internet is down, there will be no call blocking (e.g, pass-through) until power is restored and/or Internet is back online.

solution-article.title: Keep getting "Internal Server Error' at www.ultimatecallblocker.com. How can I fix this?
solution-article.description:
This issue seems to be linked to compatibility issues with secured (SSL) certificates on websites and some browser versions (e.g. Apple / Safari users).  Below are some recommendations you can follow to fix this issue:

1) First, try rebooting your device (PC, labtop, tablet of smartphone).


2) If after rebooting this problem continues, try using a different browser e.g. Chrome, Firefox, IE/Edge, etc.


3) If problem persists, try remove cookies and cache on your browser.  Below are the steps to remove cookies and cache in Chrome:
Chrome > Settings > Advanced > Privacy and Security > Clear browsing data > Select [ ] Cookies, and [  ] Cached Files, click "Clear Data"

4) If you are still getting this error, try using a different device (PC, labtop, tablet of smartphone).


If Safari is the only browser you can use, we recommend that you read this article and apply some of these tips:

https://blog.pcrisk.com/mac/12572-safari-cant-establish-a-secure-connection-how-to-fix 


If after you try the above recommendations the "internal server error" problem continues, please contact us.
solution-article.desc-un-html: This issue seems to be linked to compatibility issues with secured (SSL) certificates on websites and some browser versions (e.g. Apple / Safari users). Below are some recommendations you can follow to fix this issue: 1) First, try rebooting your device (PC, labtop, tablet of smartphone). 2) If after rebooting this problem continues, try using a different browser e.g. Chrome, Firefox, IE/Edge, etc. 3) If problem persists, try remove cookies and cache on your browser. Below are the steps to remove cookies and cache in Chrome: Chrome > Settings > Advanced > Privacy and Security > Clear browsing data > Select [ ] Cookies, and [ ] Cached Files, click "Clear Data" 4) If you are still getting this error, try using a different device (PC, labtop, tablet of smartphone). If Safari is the only browser you can use, we recommend that you read this article and apply some of these tips: https://blog.pcrisk.com/mac/12572-safari-cant-establish-a-secure-connection-how-to-fix If after you try the above recommendations the "internal server error" problem continues, please contact us.

solution-article.title: My UBlocker is not working (blocking calls or logging calls)
solution-article.description:
Please follow these 3 troubleshooting steps:


1) The first step in troubleshooting this issue is to power cycle your UBlocker unit (this fixes most issues).  Do do this, unplug the power adapter from the power outlet for a few seconds and plug it back in.

2) After you power cycled it, try making a test call to see if it shows it in the call log. It it still doesn't log calls, then the next troubleshooting step would be to make sure it is connected to the phoneline.  To check this, please sure the phone or handset connected on the "PHONE" port of the PHONE ADAPTER has dial tone.  Also, please check the USB connections on the UBlocker to make sure they are pushed in.  There should be a red light on the PHONE Adapter indicating it's receiving power from the UBLocker.  


3) After you power cycled it, try making a test call to see if it shows it in the call log. It it still doesn't log calls, then the next troubleshooting step would be to make sure it is connected to the Internet.  To do this, see if you can connect to your router (or modem) and look at your router's connected devices to see if there is a device called 'UBlocker' listed. 

If you have a UBlocker LAN or WL version ... you should see some light activity in the Ethernet port on the UBlocker; yellow, green or both.  The lights should blink showing there is activity.

If you have a UBlocker WIFI version ... Another way to find out if your device is connecting to the Internet is by using your wifi-enabled device (cell phone or tablet) and checking under Settings > WIFI under Choose a Network list of device. If you see a UBlocker listed as an available network, this means it is not connecting to the Internet (your device lost the connection or your Internet password or SSID changed). If so, you will need to redo the setup steps 1-3 as shown in the manual. 


If none of the above suggestions worked, and you still need help, please contact us.  Make sure you include your registered email or username, contact phone number and best time to reach you.
solution-article.desc-un-html: Please follow these 3 troubleshooting steps: 1) The first step in troubleshooting this issue is to power cycle your UBlocker unit (this fixes most issues). Do do this, unplug the power adapter from the power outlet for a few seconds and plug it back in. 2) After you power cycled it, try making a test call to see if it shows it in the call log. It it still doesn't log calls, then the next troubleshooting step would be to make sure it is connected to the phoneline. To check this, please sure the phone or handset connected on the "PHONE" port of the PHONE ADAPTER has dial tone. Also, please check the USB connections on the UBlocker to make sure they are pushed in. There should be a red light on the PHONE Adapter indicating it's receiving power from the UBLocker. 3) After you power cycled it, try making a test call to see if it shows it in the call log. It it still doesn't log calls, then the next troubleshooting step would be to make sure it is connected to the Internet. To do this, see if you can connect to your router (or modem) and look at your router's connected devices to see if there is a device called 'UBlocker' listed. If you have a UBlocker LAN or WL version ... you should see some light activity in the Ethernet port on the UBlocker; yellow, green or both. The lights should blink showing there is activity. If you have a UBlocker WIFI version ... Another way to find out if your device is connecting to the Internet is by using your wifi-enabled device (cell phone or tablet) and checking under Settings > WIFI under Choose a Network list of device. If you see a UBlocker listed as an available network, this means it is not connecting to the Internet (your device lost the connection or your Internet password or SSID changed). If so, you will need to redo the setup steps 1-3 as shown in the manual. If none of the above suggestions worked, and you still need help, please contact us. Make sure you include your registered email or username, contact phone number and best time to reach you.

solution-article.title: Why should I pay extra for hardware replacement?
solution-article.description:
This yearly fee covers all hardware components that may get damaged such as the UBlocker, power adapter or phone adapter.  So, in case your device stops registering calls for any reason, we will send you a replacement, and a return shipping envelop at no additional cost to you.  


If you purchase your device more than 12 months ago, without this protection, you will need to pay $75 per incident.
solution-article.desc-un-html: This yearly fee covers all hardware components that may get damaged such as the UBlocker, power adapter or phone adapter. So, in case your device stops registering calls for any reason, we will send you a replacement, and a return shipping envelop at no additional cost to you. If you purchase your device more than 12 months ago, without this protection, you will need to pay $75 per incident.

solution-article.title: My UBlocker doesn't seem to stay connected to my router. Is that okay?
solution-article.description:
That is normal for our UBlockers.  Our devices are designed to stay in 'passive' mode until they receive incoming calls.  The local connections to the local routers may disappear (e.g. not show as active devices) depending on your local router's cache time-out settings.  There is a simple test you can do in order to make sure your UBlocker is working properly.  Here it is:
1) First add your cell phone to the Blacklist
2) Wait until the UBlocker disappears from the list of active devices on your router.

3) Make a test call from your cell to your landline where the UBlocker is connected.
If the call is disconnected, your UBlocker is working properly.  If not, please let us know.

Note: Please remember to move your cell phone number back to your Whitelist.
solution-article.desc-un-html: That is normal for our UBlockers. Our devices are designed to stay in 'passive' mode until they receive incoming calls. The local connections to the local routers may disappear (e.g. not show as active devices) depending on your local router's cache time-out settings. There is a simple test you can do in order to make sure your UBlocker is working properly. Here it is: 1) First add your cell phone to the Blacklist 2) Wait until the UBlocker disappears from the list of active devices on your router. 3) Make a test call from your cell to your landline where the UBlocker is connected. If the call is disconnected, your UBlocker is working properly. If not, please let us know. Note: Please remember to move your cell phone number back to your Whitelist.

solution-article.title: How many calls are being blocked by the Global Blacklist?
solution-article.description:
We can assure you we are working very hard to grow our Blacklist database.  Please know that we are constantly collecting numbers from various government and private sources such as consumer.ftc.gov, several phone companies user blogs, social networks, and community groups. 


As of December of 2019, our global Blacklist database has 331,348 phone numbers and growing at a rate of at least 20,000 new numbers every month.  We are also growing our database with the help of our users.  Every time 3 or more of our users add a number to their User Blacklist, that number is automatically added to our global database for the rest of our users to benefit from.  We started this process in December of 2018, so we are hoping that as we grow our community of users, we will become more effective over time.


Also, we are working to launch our mobile apps for IOS (Apple) and Android phones in 2020.  We expect mobile users will help our Blacklist collection efforts a great deal.


Having said this, we want you to know that we value your patience, and will continue to work to make our UBlocker platform better.
solution-article.desc-un-html: We can assure you we are working very hard to grow our Blacklist database. Please know that we are constantly collecting numbers from various government and private sources such as consumer.ftc.gov, several phone companies user blogs, social networks, and community groups. As of December of 2019, our global Blacklist database has 331,348 phone numbers and growing at a rate of at least 20,000 new numbers every month. We are also growing our database with the help of our users. Every time 3 or more of our users add a number to their User Blacklist, that number is automatically added to our global database for the rest of our users to benefit from. We started this process in December of 2018, so we are hoping that as we grow our community of users, we will become more effective over time. Also, we are working to launch our mobile apps for IOS (Apple) and Android phones in 2020. We expect mobile users will help our Blacklist collection efforts a great deal. Having said this, we want you to know that we value your patience, and will continue to work to make our UBlocker platform better.

solution-article.title: Can I block "Out of Area" calls?
solution-article.description:
Yes, you can block "OUT OF AREA" calls by adding an entry with the text "OUT OF AREA" as "Exact Match" to the Blacklist.  


If after adding this entry to your Blacklist, you still get these calls, add it again as a "Starts with" entry. 


NOTE:  Please be advised that adding this entry to your Blacklist may block calls from cell phone callers since some mobile carriers show these calls as "Out of Area".
solution-article.desc-un-html: Yes, you can block "OUT OF AREA" calls by adding an entry with the text "OUT OF AREA" as "Exact Match" to the Blacklist. If after adding this entry to your Blacklist, you still get these calls, add it again as a "Starts with" entry. NOTE: Please be advised that adding this entry to your Blacklist may block calls from cell phone callers since some mobile carriers show these calls as "Out of Area".

solution-article.title: Why are there 4 USB ports on my Ultimate Call Blocker when only 1 USB port is used?
solution-article.description: Good question ... The hardware used for the UBlocker - LAN or LAN/WiFi version are mini-computers also known as Raspberry Pi boards.   The hardware vendors for these mini computers manufactured them with 4 USB ports and also a HDMI port - none of which are used by our application except for 1 USB port.
solution-article.desc-un-html: Good question ... The hardware used for the UBlocker - LAN or LAN/WiFi version are mini-computers also known as Raspberry Pi boards. The hardware vendors for these mini computers manufactured them with 4 USB ports and also a HDMI port - none of which are used by our application except for 1 USB port.

solution-article.title: What to do if my UBlocker freezes or stops working?
solution-article.description:
Essentially the UBlocker is a computer, so "why computers freeze?" there is no single answer -- it could be some hardware or software issue, or it can also be related to some issue external to the device such as the router or wifi software.  If your Ublocker is no logging calls in the 'call log', power cycle your Ublocker.  Just wait 2-3 minutes before powering back ON again.  This should fix the problem 99% of the time.  

If after powering OFF and ON your UBlocker it's still not logging calls online, please contact our technical support.
solution-article.desc-un-html: Essentially the UBlocker is a computer, so "why computers freeze?" there is no single answer -- it could be some hardware or software issue, or it can also be related to some issue external to the device such as the router or wifi software. If your Ublocker is no logging calls in the 'call log', power cycle your Ublocker. Just wait 2-3 minutes before powering back ON again. This should fix the problem 99% of the time. If after powering OFF and ON your UBlocker it's still not logging calls online, please contact our technical support.

solution-article.title: My UBlocker stops working and needs to power cycle every few days. Why and how can I fix this?
solution-article.description:
The main reason why the UBlocker stops registering calls is because it looses its network connectivity.  There are a number of reasons why this may happen, but they are all related to your local network.  Below are some tips to prevent these network issues:

1) If possible, assign a permanent IP to your UBlocker on your router.  This will require for you to make changes to your DHCP server settings.


2) Try setting up your UBlocker with a static IP on an external network e.g. DMZ or IP Passthrough.


3) Setup a local PC or labtop to ping your UBlocker frequently so it it will not loose its connection to the router.  


4) If you have a WIFI UBlocker, you may want to replace it with a LAN version.  Just contact us for details.
solution-article.desc-un-html: The main reason why the UBlocker stops registering calls is because it looses its network connectivity. There are a number of reasons why this may happen, but they are all related to your local network. Below are some tips to prevent these network issues: 1) If possible, assign a permanent IP to your UBlocker on your router. This will require for you to make changes to your DHCP server settings. 2) Try setting up your UBlocker with a static IP on an external network e.g. DMZ or IP Passthrough. 3) Setup a local PC or labtop to ping your UBlocker frequently so it it will not loose its connection to the router. 4) If you have a WIFI UBlocker, you may want to replace it with a LAN version. Just contact us for details.

solution-article.title: Unable to login to manage my device due to “500 internal server error”
solution-article.description:
These errors occurred due to a number of reasons but the most common one is related to compatibility with browsers between our secured server and your device(s).  Below are some suggestions in case you get "“500 internal server error” when trying to login to https://ultimatecallbocker.com.

1) Try using a different browser on your device e.g. try chrome, firefox, Edge, Internet Explorer, Opera, Safary, etc.  

2) If you tried different browsers on your device (laptop, desktop PC or tablet) and still getting these errors, try a different device e.g. tablet, laptop, desktop, etc. 


3) If you tried a different device and multiple browsers, then try connecting using a different Internet provider.


If none of this works, please contact us for further troubleshooting steps.
solution-article.desc-un-html: These errors occurred due to a number of reasons but the most common one is related to compatibility with browsers between our secured server and your device(s). Below are some suggestions in case you get "“500 internal server error” when trying to login to https://ultimatecallbocker.com. 1) Try using a different browser on your device e.g. try chrome, firefox, Edge, Internet Explorer, Opera, Safary, etc. 2) If you tried different browsers on your device (laptop, desktop PC or tablet) and still getting these errors, try a different device e.g. tablet, laptop, desktop, etc. 3) If you tried a different device and multiple browsers, then try connecting using a different Internet provider. If none of this works, please contact us for further troubleshooting steps.

solution-article.title: Can this UV-C bulb produce ozone?
solution-article.description: No, this UV-C light does not produce ozone.  A UV-C lamp at the 253.7nm wavelength will actually destroy Ozone! It also destroy bacteria, mold, and viruses (including Covid-19).
solution-article.desc-un-html: No, this UV-C light does not produce ozone. A UV-C lamp at the 253.7nm wavelength will actually destroy Ozone! It also destroy bacteria, mold, and viruses (including Covid-19).

solution-article.title: Does ultraviolet (UVC) light kill the coronavirus (Covid-19)?
solution-article.description:
Yes, UVC light has been shown to inactivate the coronavirus According to many scientific studies and tests performed by several reputable institutions 

 

Ultraviolet (UV) light is produced by the sun and by special lamps. There are three types of UV light—UVA, UVB, and UVC. UVC light includes wavelengths of light from 200 to 280 nanometers. These UVC wavelengths have the most energy of the three types of UV light. Energy from UVC wavelengths is absorbed by, and quickly destroys, the genetic material inside viruses and other microbes, rendering them inactive.

In a few laboratory experiments, UVC light has been found to destroy the ability of the SARS-CoV-2 virus to infect a host (for example, a mouse or a person), another indication that it is effective against germs. UVC light has also been shown to inactivate the genetic material in other coronaviruses.

Ref: https://www.nationalacademies.org/based-on-science/covid-19-does-ultraviolet-light-kill-the-coronavirus).
solution-article.desc-un-html: Yes, UVC light has been shown to inactivate the coronavirus According to many scientific studies and tests performed by several reputable institutions Ultraviolet (UV) light is produced by the sun and by special lamps. There are three types of UV light—UVA, UVB, and UVC. UVC light includes wavelengths of light from 200 to 280 nanometers. These UVC wavelengths have the most energy of the three types of UV light. Energy from UVC wavelengths is absorbed by, and quickly destroys, the genetic material inside viruses and other microbes, rendering them inactive. In a few laboratory experiments, UVC light has been found to destroy the ability of the SARS-CoV-2 virus to infect a host (for example, a mouse or a person), another indication that it is effective against germs. UVC light has also been shown to inactivate the genetic material in other coronaviruses. Ref: https://www.nationalacademies.org/based-on-science/covid-19-does-ultraviolet-light-kill-the-coronavirus).

solution-article.title: Is this lamp supposed to always be ON? Or only turn ON and OFF when the AC runs?
solution-article.description:
We recommend for this lamp to always be ON for 2 main reasons:


1) It will be more effective in killing germs, viruses and especially mold if kept turned ON 24x7.


2) The bulb is proven to last longer if kept ON all the time, as compared to turning it ON and OFF if connected to the fan.


So, for the installation, if you ordered the 24VAC lamp, you should get a 120v/240v to 24VAC transformer (not included with our lamp) and connect the 24VAC ballast (included with our lamp) directly to the power source.  If you have the 120VAC ballast, then just need to connect it to a 120V power source.
solution-article.desc-un-html: We recommend for this lamp to always be ON for 2 main reasons: 1) It will be more effective in killing germs, viruses and especially mold if kept turned ON 24x7. 2) The bulb is proven to last longer if kept ON all the time, as compared to turning it ON and OFF if connected to the fan. So, for the installation, if you ordered the 24VAC lamp, you should get a 120v/240v to 24VAC transformer (not included with our lamp) and connect the 24VAC ballast (included with our lamp) directly to the power source. If you have the 120VAC ballast, then just need to connect it to a 120V power source.

solution-article.title: Can I convert my No Dial Phone into a Hot Dialer (Auto Dialer or Off Hook Dialer)?
solution-article.description: Yes, you can convert your No Dial Phone into a Auto Dial (Off Hook Dialer or Hot Dialer) by purchasing one of our Mini Auto Dialers available at https://www.hqtelecom.com/autodial.  This auto dialer can fit inside any phone, and it's very easy to program to auto dial any phone number (up to 32 digits long, including and digits 0-9, and *).

solution-article.title: How to do local programing on a hotline dialer installed on a hotline phone (desk or wall-Mount).?
solution-article.description:
If you are not getting the beep sound after entering *#1234 then you will need to program it locally.  To do this, you will need to access the Hotline Dialer from inside the phone by unscrewing 3 crews in the bottom. The Hotline dialer is a small circuit attached to the back of the blank plate.  Please take a photo of the connections so you can put it back the way it was.  You will need to unplug the 2 phone wires connected to it, then connect the BLACK port to your phone line, and any of the GRAY port ports to a regular telephone with a keypad.  You then can use the keypad of this handset to re-program your hotline dialer according to instructions.  After programming it, you need to unplug it from the phone line and the phone handset, and connect it back the way it was before.
If you need help, please let us know.
solution-article.desc-un-html: If you are not getting the beep sound after entering *#1234 then you will need to program it locally. To do this, you will need to access the Hotline Dialer from inside the phone by unscrewing 3 crews in the bottom. The Hotline dialer is a small circuit attached to the back of the blank plate. Please take a photo of the connections so you can put it back the way it was. You will need to unplug the 2 phone wires connected to it, then connect the BLACK port to your phone line, and any of the GRAY port ports to a regular telephone with a keypad. You then can use the keypad of this handset to re-program your hotline dialer according to instructions. After programming it, you need to unplug it from the phone line and the phone handset, and connect it back the way it was before. If you need help, please let us know.

solution-article.title: How can I lower or turn OFF the volume on the ring bell?
solution-article.description:
On the wall phones, you can adjust the volume on the handset (use the switch on the handset), but there is no manual switch to adjust the bell ringer.  


However, there are creative ways to lower volume by using something to obstruct the ringer bell e.g. tie tape around it to lower the ring or use something that touches the bell to reduce the ring sound.  

To completely disable to bell, you can simply open it (2 screws on each side of the jack), and cut off the red and black wires connecting to the bell to the circuit board.

Watch this video to see how to disable the ring bell.
solution-article.desc-un-html: On the wall phones, you can adjust the volume on the handset (use the switch on the handset), but there is no manual switch to adjust the bell ringer. However, there are creative ways to lower volume by using something to obstruct the ringer bell e.g. tie tape around it to lower the ring or use something that touches the bell to reduce the ring sound. To completely disable to bell, you can simply open it (2 screws on each side of the jack), and cut off the red and black wires connecting to the bell to the circuit board. Watch this video to see how to disable the ring bell.

solution-article.title: I can't program the dialer remotely. Please advice
solution-article.description:
Sometimes, remote programming does not work due to some interference or compatibility issue on the telecom equipment that exists in the network you are using from the phone you are dialing out to the line where the dialer is connected to.  In case the remote programming does not work, you may need to program your dialer locally. To do this, follow these steps:
1) Open the case to access the dialer, 
2) Unplug the 2 rj11 jacks connecting the dialer to the phone (please take note of these connections so you can plug it back after you program your dialer).
3) Connect the phone line in the LINE (black) jack and any handset on the TEL1 or TEL2.  
4) Then follow programming steps 1 to 5 in this manual to program your number:
https://www.hqtelecom.com/911dialer-manual.pdf
If you still unable to program your dialer, please contact us.
solution-article.desc-un-html: Sometimes, remote programming does not work due to some interference or compatibility issue on the telecom equipment that exists in the network you are using from the phone you are dialing out to the line where the dialer is connected to. In case the remote programming does not work, you may need to program your dialer locally. To do this, follow these steps: 1) Open the case to access the dialer, 2) Unplug the 2 rj11 jacks connecting the dialer to the phone (please take note of these connections so you can plug it back after you program your dialer). 3) Connect the phone line in the LINE (black) jack and any handset on the TEL1 or TEL2. 4) Then follow programming steps 1 to 5 in this manual to program your number: https://www.hqtelecom.com/911dialer-manual.pdf If you still unable to program your dialer, please contact us.

solution-article.title: How to block 411 and 1411 calls?
solution-article.description:
Please reset the unit following feature # 7 in the manual (http://hqtelecom.com/Outgoing-Call-Blocker-Manual-FAQs.pdf). 

If you can program your call blocker to block all calls that start with 1 or 4 (simplest programming), then program feature # 5 to 01.   At this time, you should not be able to make any outgoing calls that require more than 1 digit.  Then, you should add the prefixes that you want to allow using feature # 4 (code 1034).  We recommend adding:
skip 1
2#
3#
skip 4
5#
6#
7#
8#
9#
Remember you can always bypass restrictions by entering 102222.

If you NEED to allow calls that start with 1 (except 14xx) and 4 (except 41x), then follow the steps below:

Program feature # 5 to 02.  At this time, you should not be able to make any calls that require dialing a number that is longer than 2 digits e.g. 411, etc.  After you confirm that you are not able to make any outgoing calls to 411 or 1411, then you may want to add the prefixes you like to allow using feature # 4.
We would recommend adding: 
2# -- to allow all calls that start with 2
3# -- to allow all calls that start with 3
5# -- to allow all calls that start with 5
6# -- to allow all calls that start with 6
7# -- to allow all calls that start with 7
8# -- to allow all calls that start with 8
9# -- to allow all calls that start with 9

If you wish to allow calls that start with 40,42,43,44,45,46,47,48 or 49, then add these:
40#
Skip 41 since you want to block 411
42#
43#
44#
45#
46#
47#
48#
49#

If you NEED to allow calls that start with 11,12,13,15, 16,17,18, or 19 (except 14), then add:
11#
12#
13#
Skip 14
15#
16#
17#
18#
19#

If you still need help, please contact us.
solution-article.desc-un-html:
Please reset the unit following feature # 7 in the manual (http://hqtelecom.com/Outgoing-Call-Blocker-Manual-FAQs.pdf).
If you can program your call blocker to block all calls that start with 1 or 4 (simplest programming), then program feature # 5 to 01. At this time, you should not be able to make any outgoing calls that require more than 1 digit. Then, you should add the prefixes that you want to allow using feature # 4 (code 1034). We recommend adding: skip 1 2# 3# skip 4 5# 6# 7# 8# 9# Remember you can always bypass restrictions by entering 102222.
If you NEED to allow calls that start with 1 (except 14xx) and 4 (except 41x), then follow the steps below:
Program feature # 5 to 02. At this time, you should not be able to make any calls that require dialing a number that is longer than 2 digits e.g. 411, etc. After you confirm that you are not able to make any outgoing calls to 411 or 1411, then you may want to add the prefixes you like to allow using feature # 4. We would recommend adding: 2# -- to allow all calls that start with 2 3# -- to allow all calls that start with 3 5# -- to allow all calls that start with 5 6# -- to allow all calls that start with 6 7# -- to allow all calls that start with 7 8# -- to allow all calls that start with 8 9# -- to allow all calls that start with 9
If you wish to allow calls that start with 40,42,43,44,45,46,47,48 or 49, then add these: 40# Skip 41 since you want to block 411 42# 43# 44# 45# 46# 47# 48# 49# If you NEED to allow calls that start with 11,12,13,15, 16,17,18, or 19 (except 14), then add: 11# 12# 13# Skip 14 15# 16# 17# 18# 19#
If you still need help, please contact us.

solution-article.title: Programming 4 cards (sequences)
solution-article.description:
Below are the steps to program 4 sequences using HQTelecom's Calling Card Auto Dilaer

Each number set (A-L) can consist of any combination of digits 1-9, #, except asterisk (*)

Sequence 1, has numbers A, B & C

sequence 2: D, E, F

sequence 3: G, H, I

Sequence 4: J, K, L

Sequence 1 will use 1 as a trigger, after lifting the handset (going off hook)

Sequence 2 will use 2 as a trigger, after lifting the handset (going off hook)

Sequence 3 will use 3 as a trigger, after lifting the handset (going off hook)

Sequence 4 will use 4 as a trigger, after lifting the handset (going off hook)


Programming:


***8888        ; login

*00**            ; reset

Sequence 1:

*01*1*A**     ; enter 1st number (or access number) - any combination of digits 1-9, #, except asterisk (*)

*02*1*B*C** ; enter 2nd number (or PIN) and 3rd number (or destination number) - any combination 1-9, #

*03*1*1*       ; trigger for sequence # 1 (1 - 4)

*06*1*99**    ; delay between 1st number and 2nd number (99 = 10 seconds)

*08*1*20**    ; delay between 2nd number and 3rd number (20 = 2 seconds)

*11*1*0**      ; disable IVR (voice) detection

*10*1*1**      ; enable 2nd number (or PIN)

Sequence 2:

*01*2*D**

*02*2*E*F**

*03*2*2*

*06*2*99**

*08*2*50**

*11*2*0**

*10*2*1**

Sequence 3:

*01*3*G**

*02*3*H*I**

*03*3*3*

*06*3*99**

*08*3*50**

*11*3*0**

*10*3*1**

Sequence 4:

*01*4*J**

*02*4*K*L**

*03*4*4*

*06*4*99**

*08*4*50**

*11*4*0**

*10*4*1**

*15*1234** ; enable sequence triggers 1-4


Note: this programming will dial the sequences followed by the trigger.

For example, if you go off hook and press 1, the dialer will dial number A, wait 10 seconds, B, wait 2 seconds, then C, then 1

Tip: You may want to end the 3rd number with pound (#) to prevent any issues with the trigger.

Product link


Manual

sequence 2: D, E, F
sequence 3: G, H, I

Sequence 1 will use 1 as a trigger, after lifting the handset (going off hook)
Sequence 2 will use 2 as a trigger, after lifting the handset (going off hook)

Sequence 4 will use 4 as a trigger, after lifting the handset (going off hook)

Sequence 2:
*01*2*D**
*02*2*E*F**
*03*2*2*
*06*2*99**

*11*2*0**
*10*2*1**
